approach: Model a classical dining chair with four legs, a seat, a backrest frame, and horizontal slats. Use cylinders for legs and stretchers, cubes for the seat and slats, hull operations for smooth transitions, and rotations to create the angled back. The design will feature tapered legs and a ladder-back style.

steps.description: Create the four tapered legs using cylinders with different top and bottom radii
Add the seat as a slightly rounded rectangular slab positioned on top of the legs
Create the vertical back posts (continuation of rear legs extending upward)
Add horizontal back slats (3-4 slats) connecting the back posts
Create front and side stretchers (support bars between legs) for stability
Apply slight rotation to back posts for ergonomic angle
Round edges of seat and slats using minkowski with small sphere

notes: The chair design requires careful attention to proportions and angles. The back should lean at approximately 10-15 degrees for comfort. Tapered legs (wider at top, narrower at bottom) add visual elegance. Use $fn=50-100 for smooth cylindrical components. Stretchers between legs should be positioned about 1/3 up from the floor for structural realism. The seat can have subtle rounding on edges using minkowski. Consider using modules for repeated elements like legs.
